# htmlshare agent instructions

htmlshare is an AI-first service for publishing generated HTML files and sharing them with humans.

Production base URL:

```text
https://share.metricauno.com
```

Use it when an assistant has generated an HTML file, prototype, invoice, market scan, or other static HTML/CSS/JS bundle and needs to create a shareable URL.

## Fast path

1. Register with `/api/ai/signup`.
2. If the email is new, store the returned temporary `hsk_...` token. It is valid for 24 hours.
3. The user must open the email and press the confirmation button. The token is then extended to 1 year.
4. If the email already exists, no token is returned to the agent. A token is emailed to the account owner, or the user must provide an existing token.
5. Use the `hsk_...` token as bearer auth and POST an HTML bundle to `/api/publish`.

## Register

For a human user, open:

```text
https://share.metricauno.com
```

Sign in with Google or request an email magic link.

For an automated flow, call:

```http
POST https://share.metricauno.com/api/ai/signup
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "name": "User Name",
  "agent": "claude",
  "intent": "publish-html-files"
}
```

The email address must be confirmed before durable automation is enabled. Unconfirmed automatically-created accounts and their content may be deleted after 24 hours.

If the email is new or not yet confirmed, the signup response returns a temporary agent token:

```json
{
  "api_key": "hsk_...",
  "token_expires_at": "2026-05-29T10:00:00Z",
  "confirmation_sent": true
}
```

The token works for 24 hours. The email confirmation link opens a browser page with a confirmation button. The account is not confirmed by link prefetch alone. When the user presses the button, the temporary token is extended to 1 year.

If the email already belongs to a confirmed account, the signup response does not return a token:

```json
{
  "existing_account": true,
  "token_sent": true,
  "next": "This email is already registered. A new agent token has been sent to the account owner. The LLM must ask the user to provide/store that token."
}
```

In that case, ask the user to copy the token from email or provide an existing `hsk_...` token. Do not attempt to register the same confirmed email again.

## Get an agent token

There are two supported ways to create the `hsk_...` agent token.

### Browser flow

1. Open `https://share.metricauno.com/app/`.
2. Sign in with Google or email magic link.
3. Go to `Agent keys`.
4. Click `Create key`.
5. Copy the token. It starts with `hsk_`.

### Agent flow for a confirmed browser session

If the user is already signed in and confirmed in a browser/session, an agent can create a token with that session cookie:

```http
POST https://share.metricauno.com/api/api-keys
Content-Type: application/json
Cookie: htmlshare_session=...

{
  "name": "Claude Cowork"
}
```

Successful response:

```json
{
  "api_key": "hsk_...",
  "prefix": "hsk_..."
}
```

If the response is `403 email confirmation required`, the account is not confirmed. If the response is `401 login required`, the session cookie is missing.

Use the token as:

```http
Authorization: Bearer hsk_...
```

Tokens are only shown once. Store them in the agent's secret store.

## Publish without MCP

Use the direct HTTP API when the assistant can make a POST request but cannot run MCP.

```http
POST https://share.metricauno.com/api/publish
Authorization: Bearer hsk_...
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "title": "European market scan",
  "visibility": "recipients",
  "require_registration": true,
  "ttl_seconds": 86400,
  "files": {
    "index.html": "<!doctype html><html><head><link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"style.css\"></head><body><main><h1>Market scan</h1></main><script src=\"app.js\"></script></body></html>",
    "style.css": "body{font-family:system-ui;margin:40px}",
    "app.js": "console.log('ready')"
  },
  "share": {
    "emails": ["reader@example.com", "@example.com"],
    "message": "Here is the HTML file."
  }
}
```

Successful response:

```json
{
  "id": "pub_...",
  "slug": "european-market-scan-...",
  "url": "https://share.metricauno.com/f/european-market-scan-.../",
  "shares": []
}
```

The `files` object is a map of filename to text content. Include `index.html` for the main page. CSS, JavaScript, SVG, JSON and other text assets can be included as additional keys.

Supported visibility values:

```text
private
magic
recipients
public
```

Recipient targets can be full email addresses or a domain allow-list entry in the form `@example.com`.

Recommended default for private sharing:

```json
{
  "visibility": "recipients",
  "require_registration": true
}
```

## Share an existing file

```http
POST https://share.metricauno.com/api/share
Authorization: Bearer hsk_...
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "slug": "european-market-scan-...",
  "emails": ["reader@example.com"],
  "message": "Please review this file."
}
```

## OpenAPI

The OpenAPI document is available at:

```text
https://share.metricauno.com/api
https://share.metricauno.com/api/openapi.json
```

## MCP

Remote MCP endpoint:

```text
https://share.metricauno.com/mcp
```

Suggested environment:

```text
HTMLSHARE_URL=https://share.metricauno.com
HTMLSHARE_TOKEN=hsk_...
```

Use the same `hsk_...` agent key as the bearer token for MCP.

## Cowork setup

In Cowork, allow the htmlshare domain before trying to call the API:

```text
Settings / Capabilities / Additional allowed domains
```

Add:

```text
share.metricauno.com
```

After changing allowed domains, open a new conversation. Existing conversations may not pick up the updated capability settings.

## Operational notes

- Do not publish secrets, credentials, private keys, or sensitive personal data inside HTML files.
- HTML files may include JavaScript, but the recipient should only receive content intended for them.
- Abuse can be reported from the shared page.
- Access logs may record IP address, timestamp, path, user agent and authenticated email when available.
- If comments are disabled in the current version, publishing and sharing still work through API and MCP.
